initialize_agent.reason: Replaced by LangGraph agent constructors — LangChain no longer owns agent orchestration
initialize_agent.replacement_example:
from langgraph.prebuilt import create_react_agent
agent = create_react_agent(llm, tools)
initialize_agent.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for langchain 0.3.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

ConversationBufferMemory.note: Moved to langchain_community package — requires pip install langchain-community

LLMChain.reason: Replaced by LCEL (LangChain Expression Language) pipe syntax
LLMChain.replacement_example:
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate
chain = ChatPromptTemplate.from_template('{input}') | llm

openai.ChatCompletion.create.reason: Complete SDK rewrite — class-based API replaced by client instance methods
openai.ChatCompletion.create.replacement:
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI()
client.chat.completions.create(...)
openai.ChatCompletion.create.replacement_example:
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI(api_key='your-key')
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Hello'}]
)

openai.Completion.create.reason: Completions API replaced by chat completions — old text-in text-out pattern removed
openai.Completion.create.replacement_example:
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI()
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': prompt}]
)

openai.api_key.reason: Global state removed — pass key to client constructor instead
openai.api_key.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for openai 1.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

openai.Embedding.create.reason: Class-level API removed — use client instance
openai.Embedding.create.replacement_example:
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI()

    input='text to embed',
    model='text-embedding-3-small'

validator.reason: Replaced by field_validator decorator with @classmethod requirement

BaseModel.dict.reason: Replaced by model_dump() — method renamed for consistency

BaseModel.json.reason: Replaced by model_dump_json() — method renamed for consistency

StandardScaler.fit_transform.common_mistake: Passing y as a positional argument — y is ignored for StandardScaler

sklearn.externals.joblib.reason: joblib was removed from sklearn.externals — use joblib directly

append.reason: DataFrame.append was deprecated in 1.4.0 and removed in 2.0.0 — use pd.concat instead
append.replacement_example:
import pandas as pd
result = pd.concat([df1, df2], ignore_index=True)
append.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for pandas 2.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

mad.reason: mad() (mean absolute deviation) was removed in 2.0.0
mad.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for pandas 2.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

Int64Index.reason: NumericIndex types (Int64Index, Float64Index, UInt64Index) were removed in 2.0.0
Int64Index.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for pandas 2.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

DataFrame.swaplevel.note: i and j parameter defaults changed — must now specify explicitly
DataFrame.swaplevel.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for pandas 2.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

DataFrame.iteritems.reason: iteritems() was removed — use items() instead

DataFrame.inplace.note: inplace parameter on many DataFrame methods is deprecated and will be removed in a future version
DataFrame.inplace.reason: Copy-on-Write (CoW) makes inplace semantically unclear — operations should return new objects
DataFrame.inplace.replacement_example:
# Old pattern (deprecated):
df.reset_index(inplace=True)
# New pattern:
df = df.reset_index()
DataFrame.inplace.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for pandas 2.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

sigmoid.reason: torch.nn.functional.sigmoid deprecated in favor of torch.sigmoid
sigmoid.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for torch 2.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

cholesky.reason: torch.cholesky deprecated — moved to torch.linalg namespace
cholesky.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for torch 2.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

torch.cuda.amp.autocast.note: torch.cuda.amp.autocast is deprecated — use torch.amp.autocast('cuda') instead
torch.cuda.amp.autocast.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for torch 2.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_value_.note: Still exists but foreach parameter added in 2.0 — old code works but misses perf optimization
torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_value_.common_mistake: Using old single-tensor clip instead of batched foreach=True

on_event.note: on_event('startup') and on_event('shutdown') are deprecated — use lifespan context managers
on_event.replacement:
@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    # startup
    yield
    # shutdown
app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)
on_event.replacement_example:
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from fastapi import FastAPI

@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    print('Starting up')
    yield
    print('Shutting down')

app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)
on_event.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for fastapi 0.115.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

Depends.note: use_cache parameter default changed — be explicit about caching behavior
Depends.common_mistake: Assuming dependencies are recomputed per-request without use_cache=False

per_gpu_train_batch_size.reason: Deprecated in favor of per_device_train_batch_size — per-GPU sizing replaced by per-device
per_gpu_train_batch_size.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for transformers 4.40.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained.note: device_map parameter behavior changed — 'auto' now requires accelerate installed
AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained.common_mistake: Using device_map='auto' without pip install accelerate
AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained.replacement: pip install accelerate first, then model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('model', device_map='auto')
AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for transformers 4.40.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

pipeline.note: pipeline task parameter must match model architecture — LLMs commonly generate wrong task strings
pipeline.common_mistake: Using pipeline('text-generation', model='bert-base-uncased') — BERT is not a text generation model
pipeline.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for transformers 4.40.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

AutoTokenizer.note: AutoTokenizer returns a 'fast' (Rust-backed) tokenizer by default since 4.0. Some models only have slow tokenizers.
AutoTokenizer.common_mistake: Using AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('model') and assuming it returns a slow tokenizer — fast tokenizers have different method signatures for offset mapping and encoding internals
AutoTokenizer.replacement:
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('bert-base-uncased', use_fast=False)  # if you need slow tokenizer
# or handle both: tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('bert-base-uncased')  # fast by default

GPTSimpleVectorIndex.reason: Complete package restructure — all index classes moved and renamed
GPTSimpleVectorIndex.replacement_example:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader
docs = SimpleDirectoryReader('data').load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(docs)
GPTSimpleVectorIndex.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for llama_index 0.10.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

ServiceContext.reason: Replaced by Settings global configuration object

from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI
Settings.llm = OpenAI(model='gpt-4o-mini')
ServiceContext.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for llama_index 0.10.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

LLMPredictor.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for llama_index 0.10.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

SimpleDirectoryReader.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for llama_index 0.10.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

Task.output.reason: Task output handling restructured — use TaskOutput object from crew.kickoff()
Task.output.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for crewai 0.80.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

Process.sequential.note: Import unchanged but process behavior changed in 0.30.0 — tasks no longer auto-share context
Process.sequential.common_mistake: Old code assumes agents can see each others outputs without explicit context passing

Agent.note: allow_delegation default changed from True to False in 0.51.0
Agent.common_mistake: Old code assumes agents can delegate by default — must now pass allow_delegation=True explicitly
Agent.old_behavior: Agent(role='...', goal='...', backstory='...')  # delegation was True
Agent.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for crewai 0.80.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

np.bool.reason: Alias for Python built-in bool was removed — deprecated since 1.20

np.int.reason: Alias for Python built-in int was removed — deprecated since 1.20

np.float.reason: Alias for Python built-in float was removed — deprecated since 1.20

np.complex.reason: Alias for Python built-in complex was removed

np.object.reason: Alias for Python built-in object was removed

np.str.reason: Alias for Python built-in str was removed

np.string_.reason: np.string_ was renamed to np.bytes_ — the old name was confusing because it referred to byte strings, not unicode strings

Session.execute.note: Signature changed — no longer accepts legacy Query objects, requires select() constructs
Session.execute.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for sqlalchemy 2.0.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

Query.reason: Legacy Query API removed — replaced by select() construct for all queries
Query.replacement_example:
from sqlalchemy import select
stmt = select(MyModel).where(MyModel.id == 1)
result = session.execute(stmt).scalar_one_or_none()
Query.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for sqlalchemy 2.0.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

Engine.execute.reason: Removed connectionless execution — use connection context manager
Engine.execute.replacement:
with engine.connect() as conn:
    result = conn.execute(text('SELECT * FROM table'))
Engine.execute.replacement_example:
from sqlalchemy import text
with engine.connect() as conn:
    result = conn.execute(text('SELECT * FROM users'))
    rows = result.fetchall()
Engine.execute.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for sqlalchemy 2.0.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

declarative_base.reason: declarative_base() moved from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative to sqlalchemy.orm; DeclarativeBase is the preferred SQLAlchemy 2.0 style
declarative_base.old_import:
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
Base = declarative_base()
declarative_base.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for sqlalchemy 2.0.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Hello'}]
)

anthropic.Client.reason: Old Client class replaced by Anthropic class

from anthropic import Anthropic

MessageGraph.reason: Replaced by StateGraph with MessagesState — message-only graphs use the same StateGraph

from langgraph.graph import StateGraph

langgraph.checkpoint.MemorySaver.note: MemorySaver import path changed from langgraph.checkpoint to langgraph.checkpoint.memory; graph.compile() itself is still valid.

END.note: Still exists but must be imported explicitly — not a global constant
END.common_mistake: Using END without importing it from langgraph.graph

pinecone.init.reason: Global init pattern removed — use Pinecone client class with explicit credentials
pinecone.init.replacement:
from pinecone import Pinecone
pc = Pinecone(api_key='...')
pinecone.init.replacement_example:
from pinecone import Pinecone
pc = Pinecone(api_key='your-key')
index = pc.Index('my-index')

pinecone.create_index.reason: Module-level functions removed — use client instance methods with ServerlessSpec
pinecone.create_index.replacement_example:
from pinecone import Pinecone, ServerlessSpec
pc = Pinecone(api_key='...')
pc.create_index(
    name='my-index',
    dimension=1536,
    spec=ServerlessSpec(cloud='aws', region='us-east-1')
)

pinecone.Index.reason: Module-level class access removed — must go through client instance

Client.reason: Generic Client removed — must use specific EphemeralClient (in-memory) or PersistentClient (disk) or HttpClient (server)
Client.replacement_example:
import chromadb
# For persistent storage:
client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path='./chroma_db')
# For in-memory testing:
client = chromadb.EphemeralClient()
# For remote server:
client = chromadb.HttpClient(host='localhost', port=8000)
Client.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for chromadb 0.4.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

Settings.note: Settings class restructured — chroma_db_impl was removed from common persistent-client setup
Settings.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for chromadb 0.4.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

Collection.add.note: embeddings parameter renamed in some contexts — ensure using current parameter names
Collection.add.common_mistake: Passing positional arguments instead of keyword arguments
Collection.add.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for chromadb 0.4.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

tensorflow.keras.replacement_example:
import keras
from keras import layers
model = keras.Sequential([
    layers.Dense(64, activation='relu'),
    layers.Dense(10, activation='softmax')
])
tensorflow.keras.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for keras 3.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

keras.optimizers.Adam.note: Optimizer import path simplified — use keras.optimizers directly
keras.optimizers.Adam.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for keras 3.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

model.fit_generator.reason: fit_generator removed — use model.fit which now accepts generators natively
model.fit_generator.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for keras 3.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

MotorClient.reason: Motor wraps PyMongo for async usage — every database operation returns a coroutine that must be awaited
MotorClient.note: Requires asyncio event loop — every database operation returns a coroutine
MotorClient.common_mistake: Mixing synchronous PyMongo calls inside async structures causes event loop blocking or timeout bugs
MotorClient.replacement_example:
from motor.motor_asyncio import AsyncIOMotorClient
client = AsyncIOMotorClient('mongodb://localhost:27017')
db = client.mydb
# ALL operations must be awaited:
doc = await db.collection.find_one({'key': 'value'})

motor.motor_asyncio.AsyncIOMotorClient.reason: The canonical async MongoDB client — import path is stable across Motor 3.x
motor.motor_asyncio.AsyncIOMotorClient.note: Import path is stable but ALL operations must be awaited
motor.motor_asyncio.AsyncIOMotorClient.common_mistake: Forgetting to await find(), insert_one(), etc. — returns coroutine object instead of result
motor.motor_asyncio.AsyncIOMotorClient.replacement_example:
import asyncio
from motor.motor_asyncio import AsyncIOMotorClient

async def main():
    client = AsyncIOMotorClient('mongodb://localhost:27017')
    db = client.test_db
    await db.collection.insert_one({'name': 'test'})
    doc = await db.collection.find_one({'name': 'test'})
    print(doc)

asyncio.run(main())

pymongo.MongoClient.reason: PyMongo is the synchronous MongoDB driver — commonly confused with Motor in async contexts
pymongo.MongoClient.note: PyMongo is synchronous — do NOT use inside async frameworks like FastAPI/aiohttp
pymongo.MongoClient.common_mistake: Using pymongo.MongoClient inside FastAPI endpoints blocks the event loop — causes timeouts under concurrent load
pymongo.MongoClient.old_usage:
from pymongo import MongoClient
client = MongoClient('mongodb://localhost:27017')
# Inside a FastAPI async endpoint — BLOCKS
pymongo.MongoClient.replacement_example:
# WRONG — blocks event loop in FastAPI:
from pymongo import MongoClient
client = MongoClient()

# CORRECT — async-safe:
from motor.motor_asyncio import AsyncIOMotorClient
client = AsyncIOMotorClient()

tf.Session.reason: TF2 uses eager execution by default — Sessions are no longer needed
tf.Session.replacement:
# Just call the operation directly — eager execution
result = my_operation(inputs)
tf.Session.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for tensorflow 2.16.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

tf.placeholder.reason: Placeholders removed — use tf.function with input signatures or Keras Input layers
tf.placeholder.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for tensorflow 2.16.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

tf.contrib.reason: Entire tf.contrib namespace removed — functions moved to core TF, TF Addons, or removed entirely
tf.contrib.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for tensorflow 2.16.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

tf.compat.v1.note: Compatibility module still exists but signals TF1 code — should be migrated to TF2 native
tf.compat.v1.common_mistake: Using tf.compat.v1.disable_eager_execution() as a permanent fix instead of migrating

scipy.misc.imsave.reason: Image IO functions removed — use imageio or PIL
scipy.misc.imsave.source_note: Curated API-drift signature for scipy 1.14.x; verify against official migration docs before marking safe_fix.

scipy.misc.imresize.reason: Image resize removed — use PIL or skimage

weaviate.Client.reason: Weaviate Python client v4 replaced the old v3 Client construction flow with explicit connection helpers and the WeaviateClient class.

recreate_collection.note: recreate_collection is discouraged for production migrations because it deletes existing collections before creating them; explicit existence checks, delete, and create calls are safer.
